title: Baseline noise-floor control

queries: ml experiment noise floor control rerun protocol single seed variance
deep learning reproducibility control rerun noise floor screening variance

papers.title: N/A
papers.claim: This is a control protocol candidate rather than a literature-derived modeling idea.
papers.relevance: Used to quantify representative-task variance under the current Stage 3 screening stack.

evidence.why_now: EXP078 and EXP081 both produced informative but non-promotable outcomes near the strict baseline boundaries. Before launching more structural families, Stage 3 needs a direct estimate of baseline rerun spread under the current 512-step screening protocol.

hypothesis: Rerunning the unchanged refreshed baseline under the exact same 512-step Stage 3 screening protocol will reveal the current noise floor for user-churn, user-ltv, and item-incoterms. This control bundle is not expected to improve performance; it is expected to bound interpretation error.
notes: Control only. Do not treat this as a modeling candidate.
Purpose: estimate representative-task variance under the active screening protocol before interpreting additional near-boundary deltas.
